text:
NAKSHATRA PURVA ASHADA AND THE DEITIES
G O D VA R U N A
Om let us meditate on the Great Lord Varuna. All the
waters in the world are his reflection. May that glorious
Varuna, whose form is a beautiful luminous blue,
inspire and illume our mind and understanding.
A P A H
Apah brings health and medicine, drives away and
cures all madaies. A better healer than any physician.
Swami Vivekananda says that apah is the Divine Water.

Nakshatras- PURVA ASHADA (Early Victory or The Undefeated)THE INVINCIBLE STAR
o Western star name: δ and ε Sagittarii (Delta Sagittari)
o Lord: Shukra (Venus)
o Symbol: Elephant tusk, fan, winnowing basket Indian zodiac: 13°20' - 26°40'
Dhanus
o Western zodiac 9°20' - 22°40' Capricorn
Deities : Apah the god of water, Varuna the god of rains
Symbols: Fan, Winnowing Basket
Animal symbols: Male Monkey
Varchograhana shakti: the power of invigorating.
Hand Fan
Poorva Ashadha is ruled by the Waters. Poorva Ashadha provides us additional energy for our
efforts.
